## Analysis

### What We Found
Both original and simplified detected exactly 13 faces on this image.
All 13 box coordinates are identical between original and simplified.
The simplification did NOT change any detections.

### The Pattern
**Original and Baseline** boxes are ordered by largest face area first:
  Box 1: [804.32, 439.74, 832.40, 477.19]
         size = (832.40-804.32) × (477.19-439.74) = 28.08 × 37.45 = 1051px²  ← LARGEST
  Box 2: [549.01, 538.46, 570.47, 565.82]
         size = (570.47-549.01) × (565.82-538.46) = 21.46 × 27.36 = 587px²
  Box 3: [661.36, 527.03, 683.73, 553.17]
         size = (683.73-661.36) × (553.17-527.03) = 22.37 × 26.13 = 584px²
  Pattern: descending area → largest face comes first

**Simplified** boxes have 5 columns [x1, y1, x2, y2, confidence]
and are ordered by highest confidence score first:
  Box 1: confidence = 0.99998  ← HIGHEST
  Box 2: confidence = 0.99991
  Box 3: confidence = 0.99969
  ...
  Box 13: confidence = 0.70035 ← LOWEST
  Pattern: descending confidence → most confident detection first

### Root Cause
NOT a simplification bug.
The validator compares boxes position by position:
  orig_boxes[0] vs simp_boxes[0]
  orig_boxes[1] vs simp_boxes[1]

But the orders are different so it compares wrong boxes against each other.
This caused the 958 pixel difference - it was comparing box 1 from original
against box 1 from simplified which are completely different boxes.

### Conclusion
Simplification is correct. P-Net NMS cross scale removal does not
change any detections. Validator needs to sort both outputs by the
same key before comparing.

### Fix
Sort both by x1 coordinate before comparing:
  orig_sorted = orig_boxes[np.argsort(orig_boxes[:, 0])]
  simp_sorted = simp_boxes_arr[np.argsort(simp_boxes_arr[:, 0])]
  diff = np.abs(orig_sorted - simp_sorted)
